| Scope | Focused enough for a single plan â€” not covering multiple independent subsystems |
| Architecture | Units with clear boundaries, well-defined interfaces, independently understandable and testable |

## CRITICAL

Look especially hard for:
- Any TODO markers or placeholder text
- Sections saying "to be defined later" or "will spec when X is done"
- Sections noticeably less detailed than others
- Units that lack clear boundaries or interfaces â€” can you understand what each unit does without reading its internals?
- Consistency between section 3 (commands), section 7 (skills), and section 12 (phasing) â€” do all deliverables line up?
- Whether the Remotion API usage aligns with how Remotion actually works (compositions, useCurrentFrame, interpolate, TransitionSeries)

## Output Format

## Spec Review

**Status:** Approved | Issues Found

**Issues (if any):**
- [Section X]: [specific issue] - [why it matters]

**Recommendations (advisory):**
- [suggestions that don't block approval]

content.content:
1â†’# [20] Remotion Skill Design
     2â†’
     3â†’> **Status:** Complete â€” awaiting review
     4â†’> **Date:** 2026-03-19
     5â†’> **Approach:** Template-Driven Pipeline (Approach A)
     6â†’
     7â†’## 1. Overview
     8â†’
     9â†’Claude Code skill for short-form and marketing video creation with Remotion. Enables founders to generate, preview, and render branded video content directly from Claude Code using the Remotion framework â€” React components driven by frame-based animation.
    10â†’
    11â†’**Target use cases:**
    12â†’- **Social content factory:** Reels, stories, quote cards, listicles for LinkedIn/X/Instagram
    13â†’- **Marketing assets:** Product demos, testimonials, explainer videos, pitch highlights
    14â†’
    15â†’**Core principle:** Composition-per-template. Each video type is a React composition with typed props. FOS generates the props from user input + brand kit, renders locally via Remotion CLI, and optionally uploads via the existing media-handler-agent pipeline.
    16â†’
    17â†’## 2. Architecture
    18â†’
    19â†’### 2.1 Managed Remotion Project
    20â†’
    21â†’A self-contained Remotion project scaffolded once via `video:init` at a configurable path (default: `~/.founder-os/video-studio/`). The user never needs to touch it directly.
    22â†’
    23â†’```
    24â†’~/.founder-os/video-studio/
    25â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ src/
    26â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Root.tsx              # Composition registry (all templates registered here)
    27â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ templates/            # One React component per template
    28â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialReel.tsx
    29â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialQuote.tsx
    30â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialListicle.tsx
    31â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialBeforeAfter.tsx
    32â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ ProductDemo.tsx
    33â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Testimonial.tsx
    34â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Explainer.tsx
    35â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ PitchHighlight.tsx
    36â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ components/           # Shared building blocks
    37â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ TextOverlay.tsx   # Animated text with brand fonts
    38â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Logo.tsx          # Logo placement with animation
    39â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SlideTransition.tsx
    40â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ LowerThird.tsx    # Name/title bar
    41â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ NumberCounter.tsx # Animated stat counter
    42â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ ProgressBar.tsx   # Video progress indicator
    43â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ hooks/
    44â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ useBrandKit.ts    # Reads brand-kit.json, provides colors/fonts/logos
    45â†’  â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ useAsset.ts       # Resolves asset paths with validation
    46â†’  â”‚   â””â”€â”€ lib/
    47â†’  â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ timing.ts         # Duration/fps helpers, seconds-to-frames
    48â†’  â”‚       â””â”€â”€ colors.ts         # Color manipulation (darken, lighten, contrast)
    49â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ public/
    50â†’  â”‚   â””â”€â”€ stock/                # Bundled royalty-free assets
    51â†’  â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ music/            # 3-4 background tracks (upbeat, calm, energetic, minimal)
    52â†’  â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ sfx/              # Transition sounds (whoosh, pop, click)
    53â†’  â”‚       â””â”€â”€ textures/         # Subtle gradient/texture backgrounds
    54â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ assets/
    55â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ brand-kit.json        # Resolved brand configuration
    56â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ logos/                # Company logos (PNG/SVG)
    57â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ fonts/                # Custom fonts (.woff2, .ttf)
    58â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ music/                # User-supplied background tracks
    59â†’  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ sfx/                  # User-supplied sound effects
    60â†’  â”‚   â””â”€â”€ images/               # Reusable images (headshots, product shots)
    61â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ output/                   # Rendered videos land here
    62â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ remotion.config.ts
    63â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ package.json
    64â†’  â””â”€â”€ tsconfig.json
    65â†’```
    66â†’
    67â†’**Key dependencies installed:**
    68â†’- `remotion`, `@remotion/cli`, `@remotion/player` â€” core framework
    69â†’- `@remotion/skills` â€” official AI skill rules (38 rule files)
    70â†’- `@remotion/transitions` â€” built-in transition effects
    71â†’- `@remotion/layout-utils` â€” text measurement and fitting
    72â†’- `@remotion/fonts` â€” font loading and registration
    73â†’- `@remotion/media-utils` â€” audio/video duration and metadata
    74â†’
    75â†’### 2.2 Data Flow
    76â†’
    77â†’```
    78â†’User command â”€â”€> FOS skill (video-generation)
    79â†’                    â”‚
    80â†’                    â”œâ”€ 1. Parse script / content brief
    81â†’                    â”œâ”€ 2. Select template (or use --template)
    82â†’                    â”œâ”€ 3. Load brand kit from assets/brand-kit.json
    83â†’                    â”œâ”€ 4. Resolve asset paths (images, music, logos)
    84â†’                    â”œâ”€ 5. Build typed props JSON
    85â†’                    â”‚
    86â†’                    â–¼
    87â†’              Remotion CLI
    88â†’                    â”‚
    89â†’                    â”œâ”€ npx remotion still (preview frames)
    90â†’                    â”œâ”€ npx remotion render (full video)
    91â†’                    â”‚
    92â†’                    â–¼
    93â†’              output/<filename>.mp4
    94â†’                    â”‚
    95â†’                    â”œâ”€ (optional) media-handler-agent upload
    96â†’                    â””â”€ (optional) social:post publish
    97â†’```
    98â†’
    99â†’### 2.3 Layered Skill Responsibility
   100â†’
   101â†’| Layer | Handles | Source |
   102â†’|-------|---------|--------|
   103â†’| **FOS skills** | Founder workflow: content brief parsing, template selection, brand kit application, render orchestration, upload pipeline | `skills/video/*.md` |
   104â†’| **@remotion/skills** | Remotion best practices: animation APIs (`interpolate`, `spring`), `useCurrentFrame()` patterns, `<TransitionSeries>` usage, font loading, caption rendering | `node_modules/@remotion/skills/` in managed project |
   105â†’
   106â†’The agent reads both layers when working on video tasks. FOS skills define WHAT to build; official skills define HOW to build it with Remotion's API.
   107â†’
   108â†’## 3. Command Structure
   109â†’
   110â†’New `video` namespace under `/founder-os:video:*`.
   111â†’
   112â†’### 3.1 Phase 1 Commands
   113â†’
   114â†’| Command | Description | Key Arguments |
   115â†’|---------|-------------|---------------|
   116â†’| `video:init` | One-time scaffold of managed Remotion project | `--path=<dir>`, `--skip-templates`, `--skip-stock` |
   117â†’| `video:generate` | Generate a video from template + content | `--template=<name>`, `--script="..."`, `--aspect=<preset>`, `--duration=<seconds>`, `--music=<track>`, `--post`, `--platforms=<list>` |
   118â†’| `video:preview` | Render still frames at key moments | `--template=<name>`, `--frames=0,30,60`, `--props=<json>` |
   119â†’| `video:render` | Re-render an existing composition | `--composition=<id>`, `--props=<path>`, `--format=<preset>`, `--output=<path>` |
   120â†’| `video:templates` | List available templates with descriptions | `--category=social\|marketing`, `--detail` |
   121â†’| `video:brand` | Manage brand kit assets | `--set`, `--show`, `--import`, `--reset` |
   122â†’
   123â†’### 3.2 Phase 2 Commands
   124â†’
   125â†’| Command | Description | Key Arguments |
   126â†’|---------|-------------|---------------|
   127â†’| `video:voiceover` | Generate TTS voiceover for a script | `--script="..."`, `--voice=<id>`, `--provider=elevenlabs\|openai`, `--output=<path>` |
   128â†’| `video:caption` | Generate captions from audio/video | `--input=<path>`, `--style=word\|sentence\|karaoke`, `--output=srt\|embedded` |
   129â†’
   130â†’### 3.3 Phase 3 Commands
   131â†’
   132â†’| Command | Description | Key Arguments |
   133â†’|---------|-------------|---------------|
   134â†’| `video:cloud-init` | Set up Remotion Lambda for cloud rendering | `--region=<aws-region>`, `--memory=<mb>` |
   135â†’| `video:batch` | Render multiple platform variants in parallel | `--template=<name>`, `--presets=reel,square,landscape`, `--cloud` |
   136â†’| `video:template-create` | Scaffold a new custom template | `--name=<name>`, `--category=<cat>`, `--base=<existing>` |
   137â†’
   138â†’### 3.4 Pipeline Hook
   139â†’
   140â†’`social:compose` gains `--format=video` which delegates to the video namespace:
   141â†’
   142â†’```
   143â†’social:compose --format=video --template=social-reel --platforms=linkedin,instagram
   144â†’  â””â”€> video:generate --template=social-reel --script=<content-brief>
   145â†’      â””â”€> video:preview (confirm stills)
   146â†’      â””â”€> video:render (full render)
   147â†’      â””â”€> media-handler-agent (upload to platforms)
   148â†’```
   149â†’
   150â†’### 3.5 Command Frontmatter Convention
   151â†’
   152â†’All video commands follow existing FOS patterns:
   153â†’
   154â†’```yaml
   155â†’---
   156â†’description: Generate a branded video from template and content
   157â†’argument-hint: '--template=social-reel --script="..." [--aspect=reel] [--duration=15] [--music=upbeat] [--post --platforms=linkedin,x]'
   158â†’allowed-tools: ["Read", "Write", "Bash"]
   159â†’execution-mode: background
   160â†’result-format: summary
   161â†’---
   162â†’```
   163â†’
   164â†’### 3.6 Preflight Checks
   165â†’
   166â†’Video namespace preflight requirements:
   167â†’
   168â†’| Tool | Required | Check |
   169â†’|------|----------|-------|
   170â†’| Node.js >= 18 | Yes | `node --version` |
   171â†’| Remotion project | Yes | Check managed project path exists; prompt `video:init` if missing |
   172â†’| ffmpeg | Yes | `ffmpeg -version` (needed for audio/video processing) |
   173â†’| Disk space | Yes | Warn if < 500MB free in output directory |
   174â†’| Late.dev | No | Only needed if `--post` flag used |
   175â†’| TTS provider | No | Only needed for `video:voiceover` (Phase 2) |
   176â†’
   177â†’## 4. Template System
   178â†’
   179â†’### 4.1 Template Library
   180â†’
   181â†’Each template is a React composition registered in `src/Root.tsx` with a typed props interface.
   182â†’
   183â†’**Phase 1 â€” 8 starter templates:**
   184â†’
   185â†’| Template ID | Category | Default Aspect | Default Duration | Description |
   186â†’|-------------|----------|----------------|------------------|-------------|
   187â†’| `social-reel` | Social | 9:16 (1080x1920) | 15s | Animated text slides with transitions, background music, brand colors |
   188â†’| `social-quote` | Social | 1:1 (1080x1080) | 8s | Quote card with animated text reveal, author attribution, logo |
   189â†’| `social-listicle` | Social | 9:16 (1080x1920) | 20s | Numbered tips/points with slide transitions and icons |
   190â†’| `social-before-after` | Social | 9:16 (1080x1920) | 10s | Split-screen comparison with wipe transition |
   191â†’| `product-demo` | Marketing | 16:9 (1920x1080) | 30s | Screen recording frame + callouts + intro/outro |
   192â†’| `testimonial` | Marketing | 16:9 (1920x1080) | 15s | Customer quote with photo, company logo, animated text |
   193â†’| `explainer` | Marketing | 16:9 (1920x1080) | 45s | Section-based with icons, text animations, branded lower thirds |
   194â†’| `pitch-highlight` | Marketing | 16:9 (1920x1080) | 20s | Key metrics/stats with number counter animations, logo, CTA |
   195â†’
   196â†’**Phase 2 additions (with TTS/captions):**
   197â†’
   198â†’| Template ID | Category | Aspect | Description |
   199â†’|-------------|----------|--------|-------------|
   200â†’| `talking-head` | Social | 9:16 | Voiceover with animated captions (karaoke style), background visuals |
   201â†’| `story-narrated` | Social | 9:16 | TTS narration over image slideshow with word-by-word captions |
   202â†’| `explainer-voiced` | Marketing | 16:9 | Full explainer with TTS voiceover and synced subtitles |
   203â†’
   204â†’### 4.2 Props Contract
   205â†’
   206â†’Every template exports a TypeScript interface defining its input props. Example:
   207â†’
   208â†’```typescript
   209â†’// SocialReel props
   210â†’interface SocialReelProps {
   211â†’  slides: Array<{
   212â†’    headline: string;
   213â†’    body?: string;
   214â†’    image?: string;       // path relative to assets/
   215â†’  }>;
   216â†’  brandKit: BrandKit;      // injected automatically from brand-kit.json
   217â†’  music?: string;           // path to background audio, default from brand kit
   218â†’  durationPerSlide?: number; // seconds per slide, default 3
   219â†’  transition?: 'fade' | 'slide' | 'wipe' | 'flip'; // default 'slide'
   220â†’  showLogo?: boolean;       // show logo on last slide, default true
   221â†’  ctaText?: string;         // call-to-action on final slide
   222â†’}
   223â†’
   224â†’// Shared BrandKit type (used by all templates)
   225â†’interface BrandKit {
   226â†’  company: string;
   227â†’  colors: {
   228â†’    primary: string;
   229â†’    secondary: string;
   230â†’    accent: string;
   231â†’    background: string;
   232â†’    text: string;
   233â†’  };
   234â†’  fonts: {
   235â†’    heading: string;    // filename in assets/fonts/
   236â†’    body: string;
   237â†’  };
   238â†’  logo: {
   239â†’    primary: string;    // path in assets/logos/
   240â†’    dark?: string;
   241â†’    icon?: string;
   242â†’  };
   243â†’  music?: {
   244â†’    default?: string;
   245â†’    calm?: string;
   246â†’    energetic?: string;
   247â†’  };
   248â†’}
   249â†’```
   250â†’
   251â†’### 4.3 Template Selection Algorithm
   252â†’
   253â†’In the `video-templates` skill:
   254â†’
   255â†’1. If `--template=<name>` specified, use it directly
   256â†’2. Otherwise, analyze content brief:
   257â†’   - Count sections/slides â†’ listicle vs single-card
   258â†’   - Detect quotes â†’ testimonial or quote card
   259â†’   - Detect stats/numbers â†’ pitch-highlight
   260â†’   - Detect comparison language â†’ before-after
   261â†’   - Default to social-reel for short content, explainer for long content
   262â†’3. Match target platform aspect ratio to template defaults
   263â†’4. Suggest template with rationale, let user confirm before proceeding
   264â†’
   265â†’### 4.4 Composition Registration
   266â†’
   267â†’All templates are registered in `src/Root.tsx`:
   268â†’
   269â†’```tsx
   270â†’import { Composition, Folder } from "remotion";
   271â†’import { SocialReel } from "./templates/SocialReel";
   272â†’import { SocialQuote } from "./templates/SocialQuote";
   273â†’// ... all templates
   274â†’
   275â†’export const Root: React.FC = () => {
   276â†’  return (
   277â†’    <>
   278â†’      <Folder name="social">
   279â†’        <Composition
   280â†’          id="social-reel"
   281â†’          component={SocialReel}
   282â†’          width={1080}
   283â†’          height={1920}
   284â†’          fps={30}
   285â†’          durationInFrames={450}  // 15s default, overridden by calculateMetadata
   286â†’          defaultProps={defaultSocialReelProps}
   287â†’          calculateMetadata={calculateSocialReelMetadata}
   288â†’        />
   289â†’        {/* ... other social templates */}
   290â†’      </Folder>
   291â†’      <Folder name="marketing">
   292â†’        {/* ... marketing templates */}
   293â†’      </Folder>
   294â†’    </>
   295â†’  );
   296â†’};
   297â†’```
   298â†’
   299â†’Each template uses `calculateMetadata` to dynamically set duration based on props (number of slides, script length, etc.).
   300â†’
   301â†’## 5. Rendering Pipeline
   302â†’
   303â†’### 5.1 Output Format Presets
   304â†’
   305â†’| Preset | Resolution | Aspect | FPS | Codec | Use Case |
   306â†’|--------|-----------|--------|-----|-------|----------|
   307â†’| `reel` | 1080x1920 | 9:16 | 30 | H.264 | Instagram/TikTok reels |
   308â†’| `story` | 1080x1920 | 9:16 | 30 | H.264 | Stories (15s max enforced) |
   309â†’| `square` | 1080x1080 | 1:1 | 30 | H.264 | Feed posts, LinkedIn |
   310â†’| `landscape` | 1920x1080 | 16:9 | 30 | H.264 | YouTube, presentations |
   311â†’| `gif` | 800x800 | 1:1 | 15 | GIF | Lightweight social clips |
   312â†’
   313â†’### 5.2 Local Render Flow (Phase 1)
   314â†’
   315â†’```
   316â†’video:generate
   317â†’  â”‚
   318â†’  â”œâ”€ 1. Parse script, select template, resolve brand kit
   319â†’  â”œâ”€ 2. Build typed props JSON â†’ write to /tmp/fos-video-props-<id>.json
   320â†’  â”œâ”€ 3. Validate all referenced assets exist and are valid formats
   321â†’  â”‚
   322â†’  â”œâ”€ 4. Preview (3 still frames):
   323â†’  â”‚     npx remotion still <comp-id> --frame=0 --props=<props.json> --output=preview-start.png
   324â†’  â”‚     npx remotion still <comp-id> --frame=<mid> --props=<props.json> --output=preview-mid.png
   325â†’  â”‚     npx remotion still <comp-id> --frame=<end> --props=<props.json> --output=preview-end.png
   326â†’  â”‚     â””â”€ Show previews to user, confirm before full render
   327â†’  â”‚
   328â†’  â”œâ”€ 5. Full render:
   329â†’  â”‚     npx remotion render <comp-id> \
   330â†’  â”‚       --props=<props.json> \
   331â†’  â”‚       --output=output/<filename>.mp4 \
   332â†’  â”‚       --codec=h264 \
   333â†’  â”‚       --concurrency=50%
   334â†’  â”‚
   335â†’  â””â”€ 6. Report: file path, duration, file size, resolution
   336â†’        â””â”€ If --post: hand off to media-handler-agent for upload
   337â†’```
   338â†’
   339â†’**Preview-before-render** is key: rendering 3 stills takes ~5 seconds vs 30-120 seconds for a full video. Users confirm the look before committing to the full render.
   340â†’
   341â†’### 5.3 Cloud Render Flow (Phase 3)
   342â†’
   343â†’```
   344â†’video:cloud-init (one-time setup)
   345â†’  â”œâ”€ Install @remotion/lambda
   346â†’  â”œâ”€ Deploy Remotion Lambda function to AWS
   347â†’  â”œâ”€ Store Lambda function ARN in video-studio config
   348â†’  â””â”€ Test render to verify setup
   349â†’
   350â†’video:generate --cloud
   351â†’  â”œâ”€ 1-3. Same as local (parse, props, validate)
   352â†’  â”œâ”€ 4. Preview locally (stills are fast enough locally)
   353â†’  â”œâ”€ 5. Show cost estimate before confirming cloud render
   354â†’  â”œâ”€ 6. Upload props + assets to S3
   355â†’  â”œâ”€ 7. Invoke Lambda render
   356â†’  â”œâ”€ 8. Poll for completion, download result
   357â†’  â””â”€ 9. Report: file path, duration, render time, cost
   358â†’
   359â†’video:batch --presets=reel,square,landscape --cloud
   360â†’  â”œâ”€ Spawn parallel Lambda renders for each preset
   361â†’  â”œâ”€ Same composition, different resolution/aspect
   362â†’  â””â”€ Download all variants when complete
   363â†’```
   364â†’
   365â†’### 5.4 Error Recovery
   366â†’
   367â†’| Error | Recovery |
   368â†’|-------|----------|
   369â†’| Missing font | Fall back to system font (Inter/Helvetica), warn user, suggest `video:brand --set` |
   370â†’| Missing image | Render with solid-color placeholder, warn user with asset path |
   371â†’| Missing music | Render without audio, warn user |
   372â†’| Render crash | Capture Remotion stderr, parse error. Common causes: prop type mismatch, missing asset, out-of-memory. Suggest specific fix. |
   373â†’| Disk space < 500MB | Abort before render, suggest cleaning `output/` directory |
   374â†’| Node.js version | Abort with install instructions |
   375â†’| ffmpeg missing | Abort with install instructions (brew install ffmpeg / apt install ffmpeg) |
   376â†’
   377â†’## 6. Asset Management & Brand Kit
   378â†’
   379â†’### 6.1 Brand Kit Resolution Chain
   380â†’
   381â†’1. Check `assets/brand-kit.json` in the Remotion project (cached, fastest)
   382â†’2. If missing or stale, pull from FOS business context (`_infrastructure/context/active/`)
   383â†’3. If no business context found, prompt user via `video:brand --set`
   384â†’
   385â†’### 6.2 brand-kit.json Structure
   386â†’
   387â†’```json
   388â†’{
   389â†’  "company": "Acme Inc",
   390â†’  "colors": {
   391â†’    "primary": "#2563EB",
   392â†’    "secondary": "#1E40AF",
   393â†’    "accent": "#F59E0B",
   394â†’    "background": "#0F172A",
   395â†’    "text": "#F8FAFC"
   396â†’  },
   397â†’  "fonts": {
   398â†’    "heading": "Inter-Bold.woff2",
   399â†’    "body": "Inter-Regular.woff2"
   400â†’  },
   401â†’  "logo": {
   402â†’    "primary": "logos/logo-light.svg",
   403â†’    "dark": "logos/logo-dark.svg",
   404â†’    "icon": "logos/icon.svg"
   405â†’  },
   406â†’  "music": {
   407â†’    "default": "music/upbeat-corporate.mp3",
   408â†’    "calm": "music/ambient-soft.mp3",
   409â†’    "energetic": "music/high-energy.mp3"
   410â†’  }
   411â†’}
   412â†’```
   413â†’
   414â†’### 6.3 Asset Validation Rules
   415â†’
   416â†’| Asset Type | Valid Formats | Validation |
   417â†’|-----------|--------------|------------|
   418â†’| Images | PNG, JPG, SVG, WebP | Check file exists, warn if resolution < output resolution |
   419â†’| Fonts | .woff2, .ttf | Must be loadable via `@remotion/fonts` `loadFont()` |
   420â†’| Audio | MP3, WAV, AAC | Check duration. Warn if significantly shorter/longer than video |
   421â†’| Video clips | MP4, MOV, WebM | Check codec compatibility via `@remotion/media-utils` |
   422â†’| Logos | SVG (preferred), PNG | SVG preferred for resolution independence |
   423â†’
   424â†’### 6.4 Stock Assets Bundled with Scaffolding
   425â†’
   426â†’Installed during `video:init` into `public/stock/`:
   427â†’
   428â†’- **Music:** 4 royalty-free background tracks (~30-60s each, loopable)
   429â†’  - `upbeat-corporate.mp3` â€” energetic, professional
   430â†’  - `ambient-soft.mp3` â€” calm, minimal
   431â†’  - `high-energy.mp3` â€” dynamic, fast-paced
   432â†’  - `minimal-beat.mp3` â€” subtle rhythm
   433â†’- **SFX:** 3 transition sounds
   434â†’  - `whoosh.mp3`, `pop.mp3`, `click.mp3`
   435â†’- **Textures:** 4 subtle gradient backgrounds (dark, light, warm, cool)
   436â†’
   437â†’All stock assets are royalty-free and safe for commercial use.
   438â†’
   439â†’## 7. Skill Structure
   440â†’
   441â†’### 7.1 FOS Skill Files
   442â†’
   443â†’| Skill | Path | Globs | Purpose |
   444â†’|-------|------|-------|---------|
   445â†’| `video-generation` | `skills/video/video-generation/SKILL.md` | `commands/video/generate.md` | End-to-end generate workflow: parse script, select template, resolve assets, build props, invoke render |
   446â†’| `video-templates` | `skills/video/video-templates/SKILL.md` | `commands/video/generate.md`, `commands/video/templates.md` | Template selection algorithm, prop mapping rules, content-to-template matching |
   447â†’| `video-rendering` | `skills/video/video-rendering/SKILL.md` | `commands/video/render.md`, `commands/video/preview.md` | Render pipeline: CLI invocation, output format selection, preview extraction, error recovery |
   448â†’| `brand-kit` | `skills/video/brand-kit/SKILL.md` | `commands/video/brand.md`, `commands/video/generate.md` | Brand asset resolution chain, color/font/logo mapping, fallback defaults |
   449â†’
   450â†’### 7.2 Phase 2 Skill Files
   451â†’
   452â†’| Skill | Path | Purpose |
   453â†’|-------|------|---------|
   454â†’| `voiceover` | `skills/video/voiceover/SKILL.md` | TTS provider integration, voice selection, audio generation, timing sync |
   455â†’| `captions` | `skills/video/captions/SKILL.md` | Whisper transcription, SRT generation, caption styling, word-level timing |
   456â†’
   457â†’### 7.3 Official Skills Integration
   458â†’
   459â†’`@remotion/skills` is installed in the managed Remotion project (`node_modules/@remotion/skills/`). FOS skills reference it as the authoritative source for Remotion API guidance:
   460â†’
   461â†’```markdown
   462â†’# In video-generation SKILL.md:
   463â†’
   464â†’When generating or modifying template composition code, the agent has access
   465â†’to @remotion/skills rules in the Remotion project at
   466â†’{videoStudioPath}/node_modules/@remotion/skills/skills/remotion/.
   467â†’
   468â†’Key rules to consult:
   469â†’- rules/animations.md â€” useCurrentFrame(), interpolate(), spring() patterns
   470â†’- rules/compositions.md â€” Composition registration, calculateMetadata
   471â†’- rules/transitions.md â€” TransitionSeries, fade/slide/wipe/flip/clockWipe
   472â†’- rules/fonts.md â€” Font loading via @remotion/fonts
   473â†’- rules/timing.md â€” Duration calculation, fps conversion
   474â†’- rules/images.md â€” Static file references, Img component
   475â†’- rules/videos.md â€” OffthreadVideo, video trimming
   476â†’- rules/audio.md â€” Audio component, volume control
   477â†’- rules/subtitles.md â€” Caption rendering (Phase 2)
   478â†’- rules/voiceover.md â€” TTS integration (Phase 2)
   479â†’
   480â†’IMPORTANT: All animations MUST use useCurrentFrame() + interpolate().
   481â†’Never use CSS transitions or Tailwind animation classes â€” they do not
   482â†’render correctly in Remotion.
   483â†’```
   484â†’
   485â†’### 7.4 Skill Execution Flow (video:generate)
   486â†’
   487â†’```
   488â†’1. Parse arguments (--template, --script, --aspect, --duration, --music, --post)
   489â†’2. Read skills:
   490â†’   a. _infrastructure/preflight/SKILL.md â€” validate Node, ffmpeg, project exists
   491â†’   b. skills/video/video-generation/SKILL.md â€” main workflow
   492â†’   c. skills/video/video-templates/SKILL.md â€” template selection
   493â†’   d. skills/video/brand-kit/SKILL.md â€” brand resolution
   494â†’   e. skills/video/video-rendering/SKILL.md â€” render pipeline
   495â†’3. Business context: check _infrastructure/context/active/
   496â†’4. Step 0: Memory injection (query for "video", "brand", "content")
   497â†’5. Intelligence: check learned patterns from past video:generate runs
   498â†’
   499â†’Phase 1/3: Content Preparation
   500â†’  - Parse script into structured slides/sections
   501â†’  - Select template (algorithm or --template flag)
   502â†’  - Load brand kit
   503â†’  - Resolve all asset paths
   504â†’
   505â†’Phase 2/3: Preview
   506â†’  - Generate 3 still frames (start, middle, end)
   507â†’  - Show to user with template name, duration, resolution
   508â†’  - Wait for confirmation
   509â†’
   510â†’Phase 3/3: Render
   511â†’  - Execute full render via Remotion CLI
   512â†’  - Report output path, duration, file size
   513â†’  - If --post: trigger media-handler-agent upload
   514â†’
   515â†’Final: Memory observation logging
   516â†’```
   517â†’

   549â†’### 8.2 The social:compose Bridge
   550â†’
   551â†’When `social:compose --format=video` is invoked:
   552â†’
   553â†’1. `social:compose` takes the content brief from `ideate:draft` output (headlines, body text, key points)
   554â†’2. Reads the `video-templates` skill to select an appropriate video template based on content type and target platform
   555â†’3. Delegates to `video:generate` with the brief as script input and selected template
   556â†’4. `video:generate` runs the full pipeline (props -> preview -> render)
   557â†’5. Returns the rendered video file path
   558â†’6. `social:compose` passes the file path to `media-handler-agent` for platform upload
   559â†’7. `social:post` publishes with the video attached
   560â†’
   561â†’### 8.3 Standalone Usage
   562â†’
   563â†’Users can also call video commands directly without the social pipeline:
   564â†’
   565â†’```bash
   566â†’# Direct generation
   567â†’/founder-os:video:generate --template=explainer --script="Our product solves..." --aspect=landscape
   568â†’
   569â†’# From file
   570â†’/founder-os:video:generate --template=testimonial --script=@testimonial-brief.md
   571â†’
   572â†’# Quick social reel
   573â†’/founder-os:video:generate --template=social-reel --script="5 tips for founders" --post --platforms=linkedin
   574â†’```
   575â†’

   592â†’## 9. TTS & Captions (Phase 2)
   593â†’
   594â†’### 9.1 Voiceover Generation
   595â†’
   596â†’**Supported providers:**
   597â†’
   598â†’| Provider | Model | Strengths | Setup |
   599â†’|----------|-------|-----------|-------|
   600â†’| ElevenLabs | Multilingual v2 | Most natural, voice cloning, 29 languages | API key in env |
   601â†’| OpenAI TTS | tts-1-hd | Good quality, simple API, built into Claude ecosystem | API key in env |
   602â†’
   603â†’**Workflow:**
   604â†’
   605â†’```
   606â†’video:voiceover --script="..." --voice=<id> --provider=elevenlabs
   607â†’  â”œâ”€ 1. Send script to TTS provider API
   608â†’  â”œâ”€ 2. Receive audio file (MP3/WAV)
   609â†’  â”œâ”€ 3. Save to assets/voiceover/<filename>.mp3
   610â†’  â”œâ”€ 4. Calculate audio duration
   611â†’  â””â”€ 5. Return path + duration for use in video:generate --music=<path>
   612â†’```
   613â†’
   614â†’**Voice management:**
   615â†’- `video:voiceover --list-voices` â€” list available voices from configured provider
   616â†’- `video:voiceover --preview --voice=<id>` â€” generate 5-second sample
   617â†’- Default voice stored in `brand-kit.json` under `voiceover.defaultVoice`
   618â†’
   619â†’### 9.2 Caption Generation
   620â†’
   621â†’**Transcription pipeline** using Remotion's built-in Whisper integration (`@remotion/install-whisper-cpp`):
   622â†’
   623â†’```
   624â†’video:caption --input=voiceover.mp3
   625â†’  â”œâ”€ 1. Install whisper.cpp model if not present (one-time)
   626â†’  â”œâ”€ 2. Transcribe audio â†’ word-level timestamps
   627â†’  â”œâ”€ 3. Generate SRT file
   628â†’  â””â”€ 4. Return caption data for embedding in video
   629â†’```
   630â†’
   631â†’**Caption display styles** (using Remotion's subtitle rules):
   632â†’
   633â†’| Style | Description | Best For |
   634â†’|-------|-------------|----------|
   635â†’| `word` | Word-by-word highlight (karaoke) | Reels, TikTok-style |
   636â†’| `sentence` | Full sentence with fade timing | Professional, explainers |
   637â†’| `block` | Multi-line subtitle block | Longer narration |
   638â†’
   639â†’**Integration with templates:**
   640â†’- Phase 2 templates (`talking-head`, `story-narrated`, `explainer-voiced`) accept a `captions` prop
   641â†’- Caption component respects brand kit fonts and colors
   642â†’- Caption positioning configurable: bottom, center, top
   643â†’
   644â†’### 9.3 TTS + Caption Combined Workflow
   645â†’
   646â†’```
   647â†’video:generate --template=talking-head --script="..." --voice=rachel --captions=word
   648â†’  â”œâ”€ 1. Generate voiceover from script via TTS provider
   649â†’  â”œâ”€ 2. Transcribe voiceover audio via Whisper â†’ word timestamps
   650â†’  â”œâ”€ 3. Build template props with audio path + caption data
   651â†’  â”œâ”€ 4. Calculate video duration from audio duration
   652â†’  â”œâ”€ 5. Preview â†’ Render â†’ Output
   653â†’  â””â”€ Duration matches voiceover exactly (no manual timing needed)
   654â†’```
   655â†’
   656â†’## 10. Cloud Rendering (Phase 3)
   657â†’
   658â†’### 10.1 Remotion Lambda Setup
   659â†’
   660â†’```
   661â†’video:cloud-init --region=us-east-1
   662â†’  â”œâ”€ 1. Install @remotion/lambda
   663â†’  â”œâ”€ 2. Validate AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)
   664â†’  â”œâ”€ 3. Deploy Lambda function: npx remotion lambda functions deploy
   665â†’  â”œâ”€ 4. Create S3 bucket for assets: npx remotion lambda sites create
   666â†’  â”œâ”€ 5. Store config in video-studio/cloud-config.json:
   667â†’  â”‚     { "functionName": "...", "region": "...", "s3Bucket": "..." }
   668â†’  â”œâ”€ 6. Test render with a simple composition
   669â†’  â””â”€ 7. Report: function ARN, estimated cost per render
   670â†’```
   671â†’
   672â†’### 10.2 Cloud Render Flow
   673â†’
   674â†’```
   675â†’video:generate --cloud
   676â†’  â”œâ”€ Same steps 1-4 as local (parse, props, validate, preview locally)
   677â†’  â”œâ”€ 5. Upload site bundle to S3 (if changed since last deploy)
   678â†’  â”œâ”€ 6. Show cost estimate: "~$0.02 for a 30s video at 1080p"
   679â†’  â”œâ”€ 7. Confirm with user
   680â†’  â”œâ”€ 8. Invoke: npx remotion lambda render --function-name=<fn> ...
   681â†’  â”œâ”€ 9. Poll progress (Lambda provides render progress %)
   682â†’  â”œâ”€ 10. Download rendered file from S3
   683â†’  â””â”€ 11. Clean up temporary S3 objects
   684â†’```
   685â†’
   686â†’### 10.3 Batch Rendering
   687â†’
   688â†’```
   689â†’video:batch --template=social-reel --presets=reel,square,landscape --cloud
   690â†’  â”œâ”€ Spawn 3 parallel Lambda renders (same composition, different resolutions)
   691â†’  â”œâ”€ Each render runs independently on separate Lambda invocations
   692â†’  â”œâ”€ Download all 3 variants when complete
   693â†’  â””â”€ Report: all file paths, total render time, total cost
   694â†’```
   695â†’
   696â†’### 10.4 Cost Estimation
   697â†’
   698â†’Provide cost estimate before every cloud render:
   699â†’
   700â†’| Video Length | Resolution | Estimated Cost |
   701â†’|-------------|-----------|----------------|
   702â†’| 15s | 1080p | ~$0.01 |
   703â†’| 30s | 1080p | ~$0.02 |
   704â†’| 60s | 1080p | ~$0.04 |
   705â†’| 60s | 4K | ~$0.15 |
   706â†’
   707â†’Estimates based on Remotion Lambda pricing (AWS Lambda compute time).
   708â†’
   709â†’## 11. Custom Template Creation (Phase 3)
   710â†’
   711â†’### 11.1 Template Scaffolding
   712â†’
   713â†’```
   714â†’video:template-create --name=webinar-intro --category=marketing --base=explainer
   715â†’  â”œâ”€ 1. Copy base template as starting point
   716â†’  â”œâ”€ 2. Generate new TypeScript interface for props
   717â†’  â”œâ”€ 3. Register in Root.tsx
   718â†’  â”œâ”€ 4. Create placeholder component
   719â†’  â””â”€ 5. Open Remotion Studio for live preview editing:
   720â†’        npx remotion studio (launches localhost:3000)
   721â†’```
   722â†’
   723â†’### 11.2 Template Development Workflow
   724â†’
   725â†’1. User runs `video:template-create` to scaffold
   726â†’2. Remotion Studio opens for interactive editing (hot-reload, timeline scrubbing)
   727â†’3. User can ask Claude to modify the template code (agent reads `@remotion/skills` rules for guidance)
   728â†’4. Test renders via Studio's built-in render button
   729â†’5. Template becomes available in `video:templates` list

   731â†’## 12. Phasing Summary
   732â†’
   733â†’### Phase 1 â€” MVP (Core Video Generation)
   734â†’
   735â†’**Deliverables:**
   736â†’- `video:init` â€” project scaffolding with all dependencies
   737â†’- `video:generate` â€” full generate pipeline with template selection
   738â†’- `video:preview` â€” still frame preview before render
   739â†’- `video:render` â€” re-render with updated props
   740â†’- `video:templates` â€” template listing and details
   741â†’- `video:brand` â€” brand kit management
   742â†’- 8 starter templates (4 social + 4 marketing)
   743â†’- 4 FOS skills (video-generation, video-templates, video-rendering, brand-kit)
   744â†’- Stock asset bundle (music, SFX, textures)
   745â†’- Local H.264/GIF rendering via Remotion CLI
   746â†’- 5 output presets (reel, story, square, landscape, gif)
   747â†’- Preflight checks (Node, ffmpeg, project, disk)
   748â†’- Memory integration (observation logging)
   749â†’
   750â†’**Dependencies:**
   751â†’- Remotion v4+, Node.js 18+, ffmpeg

   753â†’
   754â†’### Phase 2 â€” TTS & Captions
   755â†’
   756â†’**Deliverables:**

   758â†’- `video:caption` â€” Whisper transcription + SRT output
   759â†’- 3 new templates (talking-head, story-narrated, explainer-voiced)
   760â†’- 2 new skills (voiceover, captions)
   761â†’- `social:compose --format=video` bridge to content pipeline
   762â†’- Caption component with 3 display styles (word, sentence, block)
   763â†’- Auto-sync: voiceover duration drives video duration
   764â†’
   765â†’**Dependencies:**
   766â†’- TTS provider API key (ElevenLabs or OpenAI)

   768â†’- Phase 1 complete
   769â†’
   770â†’### Phase 3 â€” Cloud & Custom Templates
   771â†’
   772â†’**Deliverables:**
   773â†’- `video:cloud-init` â€” Remotion Lambda deployment
   774â†’- `video:batch` â€” parallel multi-preset rendering

   785â†’## 13. Risks & Mitigations
   786â†’
   787â†’| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
   788â†’|------|--------|------------|
   789â†’| Remotion project size (~200MB with node_modules) | Disk space on founder's machine | Document size in `video:init` output, support `--path` for external drives |
   790â†’| Local render speed on weak hardware | 60s video could take 5+ minutes | Preview-first workflow. Phase 3 cloud rendering offloads compute. `--concurrency=50%` default to avoid CPU lockup |
   791â†’| Template React code errors from user modifications | Broken renders | Templates are code-reviewed before shipping. Custom templates (Phase 3) render in Studio first |
   792â†’| ffmpeg not installed | Blocking dependency | Clear install instructions in preflight failure message. Link to platform-specific guides |
   793â†’| TTS API costs (Phase 2) | Recurring cost per voiceover | Show cost estimate before generation. Cache voiceovers for re-renders |
   794â†’| `@remotion/skills` package changes | Could break FOS skill references | Pin version in package.json. Test on upgrade |
   795â†’| Remotion license | Remotion requires a license for companies with >$10M revenue | Document in `video:init` output. Most founders under threshold. Link to remotion.dev/license |
   796â†’
   797â†’## 14. Success Criteria
   798â†’
   799â†’- [ ] `video:init` scaffolds a working Remotion project in < 2 minutes
   800â†’- [ ] `video:generate` produces a branded video from script input in < 3 minutes (local, 30s video)
   801â†’- [ ] Preview workflow shows 3 stills in < 10 seconds
   802â†’- [ ] All 8 starter templates render without errors
   803â†’- [ ] Brand kit applies consistently across all templates
   804â†’- [ ] `social:compose --format=video` produces and uploads a video end-to-end (Phase 2)
   805â†’- [ ] Cloud render completes in < 60 seconds for a 30s video (Phase 3)
   806â†’- [ ] Template props are fully type-safe (TypeScript compile check)

content.text:
## Spec Review

**Status:** Approved

The spec is thorough, well-structured, and ready for planning. No TODOs, placeholders, or "TBD" markers found. All sections are detailed at a consistent level of depth.

**Issues:** None blocking.

**Recommendations (advisory):**

- **[Section 3.5]:** The `allowed-tools` example shows `["Read", "Write", "Bash"]` but `video:generate` will need to invoke Remotion CLI commands, read/write props JSON to `/tmp`, and potentially trigger `media-handler-agent`. Confirm that `Bash` is sufficient to cover the Remotion CLI calls, or whether `Edit` should also be listed. Minor point since this is an example, not a binding constraint.

- **[Section 4.2]:** The `BrandKit.fonts` interface uses bare filenames (`"Inter-Bold.woff2"`) while `BrandKit.logo` and `BrandKit.music` use relative paths (`"logos/logo-light.svg"`, `"music/upbeat-corporate.mp3"`). Consider making font paths consistent with the others (e.g., `"fonts/Inter-Bold.woff2"`) to simplify `useBrandKit.ts` resolution logic.

- **[Section 5.2, step 4]:** The `remotion still` CLI command uses `--output=preview-start.png` as a bare filename. In practice these should be written to a temp directory or `output/previews/` to avoid cluttering the project root. The skill implementation will likely handle this, but the spec could be explicit about the preview output path.

- **[Section 8.2]:** The `social:compose --format=video` bridge is listed as a Phase 2 deliverable in Section 12, but the bridge logic described in 8.2 does not inherently require TTS or captions. If the bridge only needs Phase 1 templates, consider moving it to Phase 1 to unlock the full content pipeline earlier. If intentionally gated on Phase 2 for the voiced templates, note that explicitly.

- **[Section 12, Phase 2]:** The `social:compose --format=video` bridge is listed here as a Phase 2 deliverable. Cross-reference with Section 3.4 which describes it without phase annotation. This is consistent (3.4 describes the mechanism, 12 assigns the phase), but worth confirming the Phase 2 placement is intentional given the bridge works with Phase 1 templates.

- **[Section 6.4]:** The stock asset bundle specifies royalty-free audio tracks by name (e.g., `upbeat-corporate.mp3`). The actual sourcing/licensing of these files is not in scope for this spec, but the implementation plan should include a task for sourcing or creating these assets with verifiable licenses.

- **[Section 13]:** The Remotion license risk is well documented. Consider also noting that `@remotion/install-whisper-cpp` (Phase 2) downloads a ~100-400MB model file, which is a separate disk space consideration from the 200MB project size mentioned.

**Cross-reference checks passed:**
- Section 3 commands (6 Phase 1 + 2 Phase 2 + 3 Phase 3) align with Section 12 phasing deliverables.
- Section 7 skills (4 Phase 1 + 2 Phase 2) align with Section 12 phasing deliverables.
- Template count (8 Phase 1 + 3 Phase 2) is consistent between Sections 4.1 and 12.
- Remotion API usage (`useCurrentFrame`, `interpolate`, `spring`, `Composition`, `calculateMetadata`, `TransitionSeries`, `Folder`) aligns with actual Remotion v4 API patterns.
- The `@remotion/skills` package reference and rule file paths match the real package structure.
- The preflight checks in Section 3.6 cover all dependencies listed in Section 12.

You are a spec document reviewer. Verify this spec is complete and ready for planning.

**Spec to review:** /Users/lhalicki/coding_projects/founder-workspace/docs/superpowers/specs/[20]-2026-03-18-remotion-skill-design.md

**Project context:** This is a Founder OS plugin spec. The project is a Claude Code plugin with 33 command namespaces. Skills are markdown knowledge documents, commands are markdown files with YAML frontmatter. The project uses Notion as source of truth, has existing media upload infrastructure (late-media skill, media-handler-agent), and a content pipeline (ideate:draft -> social:compose -> social:post). The Remotion spec adds a new `video` namespace.

**Key conventions to check against:**
- Commands use YAML frontmatter (description, argument-hint, allowed-tools, execution-mode, result-format)
- Skills are in `skills/<namespace>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` with name, description, globs frontmatter
- All commands follow the 3-phase execution pattern with preflight, memory injection, intelligence patterns
- Compositions reference `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` for file paths
- Templates reference the `@remotion/skills` package for Remotion API guidance

## What to Check

| Category | What to Look For |
|----------|------------------|
| Completeness | TODOs, placeholders, "TBD", incomplete sections |
| Coverage | Missing error handling, edge cases, integration points |
| Consistency | Internal contradictions, conflicting requirements |
| Clarity | Ambiguous requirements |
| YAGNI | Unrequested features, over-engineering |
| Scope | Focused enough for a single plan â€” not covering multiple independent subsystems |
| Architecture | Units with clear boundaries, well-defined interfaces, independently understandable and testable |

## CRITICAL

Look especially hard for:
- Any TODO markers or placeholder text
- Sections saying "to be defined later" or "will spec when X is done"
- Sections noticeably less detailed than others
- Units that lack clear boundaries or interfaces â€” can you understand what each unit does without reading its internals?
- Consistency between section 3 (commands), section 7 (skills), and section 12 (phasing) â€” do all deliverables line up?
- Whether the Remotion API usage aligns with how Remotion actually works (compositions, useCurrentFrame, interpolate, TransitionSeries)

## Output Format

## Spec Review

**Status:** Approved | Issues Found

**Issues (if any):**
- [Section X]: [specific issue] - [why it matters]

**Recommendations (advisory):**
- [suggestions that don't block approval]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# [20] Remotion Skill Design

> **Status:** Complete â€” awaiting review
> **Date:** 2026-03-19
> **Approach:** Template-Driven Pipeline (Approach A)

## 1. Overview

Claude Code skill for short-form and marketing video creation with Remotion. Enables founders to generate, preview, and render branded video content directly from Claude Code using the Remotion framework â€” React components driven by frame-based animation.

**Target use cases:**
- **Social content factory:** Reels, stories, quote cards, listicles for LinkedIn/X/Instagram
- **Marketing assets:** Product demos, testimonials, explainer videos, pitch highlights

**Core principle:** Composition-per-template. Each video type is a React composition with typed props. FOS generates the props from user input + brand kit, renders locally via Remotion CLI, and optionally uploads via the existing media-handler-agent pipeline.

## 2. Architecture

### 2.1 Managed Remotion Project

A self-contained Remotion project scaffolded once via `video:init` at a configurable path (default: `~/.founder-os/video-studio/`). The user never needs to touch it directly.

```
~/.founder-os/video-studio/
  â”œâ”€â”€ src/
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Root.tsx              # Composition registry (all templates registered here)
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ templates/            # One React component per template
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialReel.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialQuote.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialListicle.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialBeforeAfter.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ ProductDemo.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Testimonial.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Explainer.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ PitchHighlight.tsx
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ components/           # Shared building blocks
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ TextOverlay.tsx   # Animated text with brand fonts
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Logo.tsx          # Logo placement with animation
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SlideTransition.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ LowerThird.tsx    # Name/title bar
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ NumberCounter.tsx # Animated stat counter
  â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ ProgressBar.tsx   # Video progress indicator
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ hooks/
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ useBrandKit.ts    # Reads brand-kit.json, provides colors/fonts/logos
  â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ useAsset.ts       # Resolves asset paths with validation
  â”‚   â””â”€â”€ lib/
  â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ timing.ts         # Duration/fps helpers, seconds-to-frames
  â”‚       â””â”€â”€ colors.ts         # Color manipulation (darken, lighten, contrast)
  â”œâ”€â”€ public/
  â”‚   â””â”€â”€ stock/                # Bundled royalty-free assets
  â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ music/            # 3-4 background tracks (upbeat, calm, energetic, minimal)
  â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ sfx/              # Transition sounds (whoosh, pop, click)
  â”‚       â””â”€â”€ textures/         # Subtle gradient/texture backgrounds
  â”œâ”€â”€ assets/
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ brand-kit.json        # Resolved brand configuration
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ logos/                # Company logos (PNG/SVG)
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ fonts/                # Custom fonts (.woff2, .ttf)
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ music/                # User-supplied background tracks
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ sfx/                  # User-supplied sound effects
  â”‚   â””â”€â”€ images/               # Reusable images (headshots, product shots)
  â”œâ”€â”€ output/                   # Rendered videos land here
  â”œâ”€â”€ remotion.config.ts
  â”œâ”€â”€ package.json
  â””â”€â”€ tsconfig.json
```

**Key dependencies installed:**
- `remotion`, `@remotion/cli`, `@remotion/player` â€” core framework
- `@remotion/skills` â€” official AI skill rules (38 rule files)
- `@remotion/transitions` â€” built-in transition effects
- `@remotion/layout-utils` â€” text measurement and fitting
- `@remotion/fonts` â€” font loading and registration
- `@remotion/media-utils` â€” audio/video duration and metadata

### 2.2 Data Flow

```
User command â”€â”€> FOS skill (video-generation)
                    â”‚
                    â”œâ”€ 1. Parse script / content brief
                    â”œâ”€ 2. Select template (or use --template)
                    â”œâ”€ 3. Load brand kit from assets/brand-kit.json
                    â”œâ”€ 4. Resolve asset paths (images, music, logos)
                    â”œâ”€ 5. Build typed props JSON
                    â”‚
                    â–¼
              Remotion CLI
                    â”‚
                    â”œâ”€ npx remotion still (preview frames)
                    â”œâ”€ npx remotion render (full video)
                    â”‚
                    â–¼
              output/<filename>.mp4
                    â”‚
                    â”œâ”€ (optional) media-handler-agent upload
                    â””â”€ (optional) social:post publish
```

### 2.3 Layered Skill Responsibility

| Layer | Handles | Source |
|-------|---------|--------|
| **FOS skills** | Founder workflow: content brief parsing, template selection, brand kit application, render orchestration, upload pipeline | `skills/video/*.md` |
| **@remotion/skills** | Remotion best practices: animation APIs (`interpolate`, `spring`), `useCurrentFrame()` patterns, `<TransitionSeries>` usage, font loading, caption rendering | `node_modules/@remotion/skills/` in managed project |

The agent reads both layers when working on video tasks. FOS skills define WHAT to build; official skills define HOW to build it with Remotion's API.

## 3. Command Structure

New `video` namespace under `/founder-os:video:*`.

### 3.1 Phase 1 Commands

| Command | Description | Key Arguments |
|---------|-------------|---------------|
| `video:init` | One-time scaffold of managed Remotion project | `--path=<dir>`, `--skip-templates`, `--skip-stock` |
| `video:generate` | Generate a video from template + content | `--template=<name>`, `--script="..."`, `--aspect=<preset>`, `--duration=<seconds>`, `--music=<track>`, `--post`, `--platforms=<list>` |
| `video:preview` | Render still frames at key moments | `--template=<name>`, `--frames=0,30,60`, `--props=<json>` |
| `video:render` | Re-render an existing composition | `--composition=<id>`, `--props=<path>`, `--format=<preset>`, `--output=<path>` |
| `video:templates` | List available templates with descriptions | `--category=social\|marketing`, `--detail` |
| `video:brand` | Manage brand kit assets | `--set`, `--show`, `--import`, `--reset` |

### 3.2 Phase 2 Commands

| Command | Description | Key Arguments |
|---------|-------------|---------------|
| `video:voiceover` | Generate TTS voiceover for a script | `--script="..."`, `--voice=<id>`, `--provider=elevenlabs\|openai`, `--output=<path>` |
| `video:caption` | Generate captions from audio/video | `--input=<path>`, `--style=word\|sentence\|karaoke`, `--output=srt\|embedded` |

### 3.3 Phase 3 Commands

| Command | Description | Key Arguments |
|---------|-------------|---------------|
| `video:cloud-init` | Set up Remotion Lambda for cloud rendering | `--region=<aws-region>`, `--memory=<mb>` |
| `video:batch` | Render multiple platform variants in parallel | `--template=<name>`, `--presets=reel,square,landscape`, `--cloud` |
| `video:template-create` | Scaffold a new custom template | `--name=<name>`, `--category=<cat>`, `--base=<existing>` |

### 3.4 Pipeline Hook

`social:compose` gains `--format=video` which delegates to the video namespace:

```
social:compose --format=video --template=social-reel --platforms=linkedin,instagram
  â””â”€> video:generate --template=social-reel --script=<content-brief>
      â””â”€> video:preview (confirm stills)
      â””â”€> video:render (full render)
      â””â”€> media-handler-agent (upload to platforms)
```

### 3.5 Command Frontmatter Convention

All video commands follow existing FOS patterns:

```yaml
---
description: Generate a branded video from template and content
argument-hint: '--template=social-reel --script="..." [--aspect=reel] [--duration=15] [--music=upbeat] [--post --platforms=linkedin,x]'
allowed-tools: ["Read", "Write", "Bash"]
execution-mode: background
result-format: summary
---
```

### 3.6 Preflight Checks

Video namespace preflight requirements:

| Tool | Required | Check |
|------|----------|-------|
| Node.js >= 18 | Yes | `node --version` |
| Remotion project | Yes | Check managed project path exists; prompt `video:init` if missing |
| ffmpeg | Yes | `ffmpeg -version` (needed for audio/video processing) |
| Disk space | Yes | Warn if < 500MB free in output directory |
| Late.dev | No | Only needed if `--post` flag used |
| TTS provider | No | Only needed for `video:voiceover` (Phase 2) |

## 4. Template System

### 4.1 Template Library

Each template is a React composition registered in `src/Root.tsx` with a typed props interface.

**Phase 1 â€” 8 starter templates:**

| Template ID | Category | Default Aspect | Default Duration | Description |
|-------------|----------|----------------|------------------|-------------|
| `social-reel` | Social | 9:16 (1080x1920) | 15s | Animated text slides with transitions, background music, brand colors |
| `social-quote` | Social | 1:1 (1080x1080) | 8s | Quote card with animated text reveal, author attribution, logo |
| `social-listicle` | Social | 9:16 (1080x1920) | 20s | Numbered tips/points with slide transitions and icons |
| `social-before-after` | Social | 9:16 (1080x1920) | 10s | Split-screen comparison with wipe transition |
| `product-demo` | Marketing | 16:9 (1920x1080) | 30s | Screen recording frame + callouts + intro/outro |
| `testimonial` | Marketing | 16:9 (1920x1080) | 15s | Customer quote with photo, company logo, animated text |
| `explainer` | Marketing | 16:9 (1920x1080) | 45s | Section-based with icons, text animations, branded lower thirds |
| `pitch-highlight` | Marketing | 16:9 (1920x1080) | 20s | Key metrics/stats with number counter animations, logo, CTA |

**Phase 2 additions (with TTS/captions):**

| Template ID | Category | Aspect | Description |
|-------------|----------|--------|-------------|
| `talking-head` | Social | 9:16 | Voiceover with animated captions (karaoke style), background visuals |
| `story-narrated` | Social | 9:16 | TTS narration over image slideshow with word-by-word captions |
| `explainer-voiced` | Marketing | 16:9 | Full explainer with TTS voiceover and synced subtitles |

### 4.2 Props Contract

Every template exports a TypeScript interface defining its input props. Example:

```typescript
// SocialReel props
interface SocialReelProps {
  slides: Array<{
    headline: string;
    body?: string;
    image?: string;       // path relative to assets/
  }>;
  brandKit: BrandKit;      // injected automatically from brand-kit.json
  music?: string;           // path to background audio, default from brand kit
  durationPerSlide?: number; // seconds per slide, default 3
  transition?: 'fade' | 'slide' | 'wipe' | 'flip'; // default 'slide'
  showLogo?: boolean;       // show logo on last slide, default true
  ctaText?: string;         // call-to-action on final slide
}

// Shared BrandKit type (used by all templates)
interface BrandKit {
  company: string;
  colors: {
    primary: string;
    secondary: string;
    accent: string;
    background: string;
    text: string;
  };
  fonts: {
    heading: string;    // filename in assets/fonts/
    body: string;
  };
  logo: {
    primary: string;    // path in assets/logos/
    dark?: string;
    icon?: string;
  };
  music?: {
    default?: string;
    calm?: string;
    energetic?: string;
  };
}
```

### 4.3 Template Selection Algorithm

In the `video-templates` skill:

1. If `--template=<name>` specified, use it directly
2. Otherwise, analyze content brief:
   - Count sections/slides â†’ listicle vs single-card
   - Detect quotes â†’ testimonial or quote card
   - Detect stats/numbers â†’ pitch-highlight
   - Detect comparison language â†’ before-after
   - Default to social-reel for short content, explainer for long content
3. Match target platform aspect ratio to template defaults
4. Suggest template with rationale, let user confirm before proceeding

### 4.4 Composition Registration

All templates are registered in `src/Root.tsx`:

```tsx
import { Composition, Folder } from "remotion";
import { SocialReel } from "./templates/SocialReel";
import { SocialQuote } from "./templates/SocialQuote";
// ... all templates

export const Root: React.FC = () => {
  return (
    <>
      <Folder name="social">
        <Composition
          id="social-reel"
          component={SocialReel}
          width={1080}
          height={1920}
          fps={30}
          durationInFrames={450}  // 15s default, overridden by calculateMetadata
          defaultProps={defaultSocialReelProps}
          calculateMetadata={calculateSocialReelMetadata}
        />
        {/* ... other social templates */}
      </Folder>
      <Folder name="marketing">
        {/* ... marketing templates */}
      </Folder>
    </>
  );
};
```

Each template uses `calculateMetadata` to dynamically set duration based on props (number of slides, script length, etc.).

## 5. Rendering Pipeline

### 5.1 Output Format Presets

| Preset | Resolution | Aspect | FPS | Codec | Use Case |
|--------|-----------|--------|-----|-------|----------|
| `reel` | 1080x1920 | 9:16 | 30 | H.264 | Instagram/TikTok reels |
| `story` | 1080x1920 | 9:16 | 30 | H.264 | Stories (15s max enforced) |
| `square` | 1080x1080 | 1:1 | 30 | H.264 | Feed posts, LinkedIn |
| `landscape` | 1920x1080 | 16:9 | 30 | H.264 | YouTube, presentations |
| `gif` | 800x800 | 1:1 | 15 | GIF | Lightweight social clips |

### 5.2 Local Render Flow (Phase 1)

```
video:generate
  â”‚
  â”œâ”€ 1. Parse script, select template, resolve brand kit
  â”œâ”€ 2. Build typed props JSON â†’ write to /tmp/fos-video-props-<id>.json
  â”œâ”€ 3. Validate all referenced assets exist and are valid formats
  â”‚
  â”œâ”€ 4. Preview (3 still frames):
  â”‚     npx remotion still <comp-id> --frame=0 --props=<props.json> --output=preview-start.png
  â”‚     npx remotion still <comp-id> --frame=<mid> --props=<props.json> --output=preview-mid.png
  â”‚     npx remotion still <comp-id> --frame=<end> --props=<props.json> --output=preview-end.png
  â”‚     â””â”€ Show previews to user, confirm before full render
  â”‚
  â”œâ”€ 5. Full render:
  â”‚     npx remotion render <comp-id> \
  â”‚       --props=<props.json> \
  â”‚       --output=output/<filename>.mp4 \
  â”‚       --codec=h264 \
  â”‚       --concurrency=50%
  â”‚
  â””â”€ 6. Report: file path, duration, file size, resolution
        â””â”€ If --post: hand off to media-handler-agent for upload
```

**Preview-before-render** is key: rendering 3 stills takes ~5 seconds vs 30-120 seconds for a full video. Users confirm the look before committing to the full render.

### 5.3 Cloud Render Flow (Phase 3)

```
video:cloud-init (one-time setup)
  â”œâ”€ Install @remotion/lambda
  â”œâ”€ Deploy Remotion Lambda function to AWS
  â”œâ”€ Store Lambda function ARN in video-studio config
  â””â”€ Test render to verify setup

video:generate --cloud
  â”œâ”€ 1-3. Same as local (parse, props, validate)
  â”œâ”€ 4. Preview locally (stills are fast enough locally)
  â”œâ”€ 5. Show cost estimate before confirming cloud render
  â”œâ”€ 6. Upload props + assets to S3
  â”œâ”€ 7. Invoke Lambda render
  â”œâ”€ 8. Poll for completion, download result
  â””â”€ 9. Report: file path, duration, render time, cost

video:batch --presets=reel,square,landscape --cloud
  â”œâ”€ Spawn parallel Lambda renders for each preset
  â”œâ”€ Same composition, different resolution/aspect
  â””â”€ Download all variants when complete
```

### 5.4 Error Recovery

| Error | Recovery |
|-------|----------|
| Missing font | Fall back to system font (Inter/Helvetica), warn user, suggest `video:brand --set` |
| Missing image | Render with solid-color placeholder, warn user with asset path |
| Missing music | Render without audio, warn user |
| Render crash | Capture Remotion stderr, parse error. Common causes: prop type mismatch, missing asset, out-of-memory. Suggest specific fix. |
| Disk space < 500MB | Abort before render, suggest cleaning `output/` directory |
| Node.js version | Abort with install instructions |
| ffmpeg missing | Abort with install instructions (brew install ffmpeg / apt install ffmpeg) |

## 6. Asset Management & Brand Kit

### 6.1 Brand Kit Resolution Chain

1. Check `assets/brand-kit.json` in the Remotion project (cached, fastest)
2. If missing or stale, pull from FOS business context (`_infrastructure/context/active/`)
3. If no business context found, prompt user via `video:brand --set`

### 6.2 brand-kit.json Structure

```json
{
  "company": "Acme Inc",
  "colors": {
    "primary": "#2563EB",
    "secondary": "#1E40AF",
    "accent": "#F59E0B",
    "background": "#0F172A",
    "text": "#F8FAFC"
  },
  "fonts": {
    "heading": "Inter-Bold.woff2",
    "body": "Inter-Regular.woff2"
  },
  "logo": {
    "primary": "logos/logo-light.svg",
    "dark": "logos/logo-dark.svg",
    "icon": "logos/icon.svg"
  },
  "music": {
    "default": "music/upbeat-corporate.mp3",
    "calm": "music/ambient-soft.mp3",
    "energetic": "music/high-energy.mp3"
  }
}
```

### 6.3 Asset Validation Rules

| Asset Type | Valid Formats | Validation |
|-----------|--------------|------------|
| Images | PNG, JPG, SVG, WebP | Check file exists, warn if resolution < output resolution |
| Fonts | .woff2, .ttf | Must be loadable via `@remotion/fonts` `loadFont()` |
| Audio | MP3, WAV, AAC | Check duration. Warn if significantly shorter/longer than video |
| Video clips | MP4, MOV, WebM | Check codec compatibility via `@remotion/media-utils` |
| Logos | SVG (preferred), PNG | SVG preferred for resolution independence |

### 6.4 Stock Assets Bundled with Scaffolding

Installed during `video:init` into `public/stock/`:

- **Music:** 4 royalty-free background tracks (~30-60s each, loopable)
  - `upbeat-corporate.mp3` â€” energetic, professional
  - `ambient-soft.mp3` â€” calm, minimal
  - `high-energy.mp3` â€” dynamic, fast-paced
  - `minimal-beat.mp3` â€” subtle rhythm
- **SFX:** 3 transition sounds
  - `whoosh.mp3`, `pop.mp3`, `click.mp3`
- **Textures:** 4 subtle gradient backgrounds (dark, light, warm, cool)

All stock assets are royalty-free and safe for commercial use.

## 7. Skill Structure

### 7.1 FOS Skill Files

| Skill | Path | Globs | Purpose |
|-------|------|-------|---------|
| `video-generation` | `skills/video/video-generation/SKILL.md` | `commands/video/generate.md` | End-to-end generate workflow: parse script, select template, resolve assets, build props, invoke render |
| `video-templates` | `skills/video/video-templates/SKILL.md` | `commands/video/generate.md`, `commands/video/templates.md` | Template selection algorithm, prop mapping rules, content-to-template matching |
| `video-rendering` | `skills/video/video-rendering/SKILL.md` | `commands/video/render.md`, `commands/video/preview.md` | Render pipeline: CLI invocation, output format selection, preview extraction, error recovery |
| `brand-kit` | `skills/video/brand-kit/SKILL.md` | `commands/video/brand.md`, `commands/video/generate.md` | Brand asset resolution chain, color/font/logo mapping, fallback defaults |

### 7.2 Phase 2 Skill Files

| Skill | Path | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `voiceover` | `skills/video/voiceover/SKILL.md` | TTS provider integration, voice selection, audio generation, timing sync |
| `captions` | `skills/video/captions/SKILL.md` | Whisper transcription, SRT generation, caption styling, word-level timing |

### 7.3 Official Skills Integration

`@remotion/skills` is installed in the managed Remotion project (`node_modules/@remotion/skills/`). FOS skills reference it as the authoritative source for Remotion API guidance:

```markdown
# In video-generation SKILL.md:

When generating or modifying template composition code, the agent has access
to @remotion/skills rules in the Remotion project at
{videoStudioPath}/node_modules/@remotion/skills/skills/remotion/.

Key rules to consult:
- rules/animations.md â€” useCurrentFrame(), interpolate(), spring() patterns
- rules/compositions.md â€” Composition registration, calculateMetadata
- rules/transitions.md â€” TransitionSeries, fade/slide/wipe/flip/clockWipe
- rules/fonts.md â€” Font loading via @remotion/fonts
- rules/timing.md â€” Duration calculation, fps conversion
- rules/images.md â€” Static file references, Img component
- rules/videos.md â€” OffthreadVideo, video trimming
- rules/audio.md â€” Audio component, volume control
- rules/subtitles.md â€” Caption rendering (Phase 2)
- rules/voiceover.md â€” TTS integration (Phase 2)

IMPORTANT: All animations MUST use useCurrentFrame() + interpolate().
Never use CSS transitions or Tailwind animation classes â€” they do not
render correctly in Remotion.
```

### 7.4 Skill Execution Flow (video:generate)

```
1. Parse arguments (--template, --script, --aspect, --duration, --music, --post)
2. Read skills:
   a. _infrastructure/preflight/SKILL.md â€” validate Node, ffmpeg, project exists
   b. skills/video/video-generation/SKILL.md â€” main workflow
   c. skills/video/video-templates/SKILL.md â€” template selection
   d. skills/video/brand-kit/SKILL.md â€” brand resolution
   e. skills/video/video-rendering/SKILL.md â€” render pipeline
3. Business context: check _infrastructure/context/active/
4. Step 0: Memory injection (query for "video", "brand", "content")
5. Intelligence: check learned patterns from past video:generate runs

Phase 1/3: Content Preparation
  - Parse script into structured slides/sections
  - Select template (algorithm or --template flag)
  - Load brand kit
  - Resolve all asset paths

Phase 2/3: Preview
  - Generate 3 still frames (start, middle, end)
  - Show to user with template name, duration, resolution
  - Wait for confirmation

Phase 3/3: Render
  - Execute full render via Remotion CLI
  - Report output path, duration, file size
  - If --post: trigger media-handler-agent upload

Final: Memory observation logging
```

## 8. Content Pipeline Integration

### 8.1 Integration Points

```

### 8.2 The social:compose Bridge

When `social:compose --format=video` is invoked:

1. `social:compose` takes the content brief from `ideate:draft` output (headlines, body text, key points)
2. Reads the `video-templates` skill to select an appropriate video template based on content type and target platform
3. Delegates to `video:generate` with the brief as script input and selected template
4. `video:generate` runs the full pipeline (props -> preview -> render)
5. Returns the rendered video file path
6. `social:compose` passes the file path to `media-handler-agent` for platform upload
7. `social:post` publishes with the video attached

### 8.3 Standalone Usage

Users can also call video commands directly without the social pipeline:

```bash
# Direct generation
/founder-os:video:generate --template=explainer --script="Our product solves..." --aspect=landscape

# From file
/founder-os:video:generate --template=testimonial --script=@testimonial-brief.md

# Quick social reel
/founder-os:video:generate --template=social-reel --script="5 tips for founders" --post --platforms=linkedin
```

### 8.4 Notion HQ Integration

New entries in the Content database:

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Type | `Video` |
| Title | Video title/description |
| Status | `Draft` / `Rendered` / `Published` |
| Template | Template ID used |
| Platforms | Target platforms |
| Output URL | Local file path or published URL |
| Duration | Video duration in seconds |
| Aspect | Output preset used |
| Company | Related company (if client video) |

## 9. TTS & Captions (Phase 2)

### 9.1 Voiceover Generation

**Supported providers:**

| Provider | Model | Strengths | Setup |
|----------|-------|-----------|-------|
| ElevenLabs | Multilingual v2 | Most natural, voice cloning, 29 languages | API key in env |
| OpenAI TTS | tts-1-hd | Good quality, simple API, built into Claude ecosystem | API key in env |

**Workflow:**

```
video:voiceover --script="..." --voice=<id> --provider=elevenlabs
  â”œâ”€ 1. Send script to TTS provider API
  â”œâ”€ 2. Receive audio file (MP3/WAV)
  â”œâ”€ 3. Save to assets/voiceover/<filename>.mp3
  â”œâ”€ 4. Calculate audio duration
  â””â”€ 5. Return path + duration for use in video:generate --music=<path>
```

**Voice management:**
- `video:voiceover --list-voices` â€” list available voices from configured provider
- `video:voiceover --preview --voice=<id>` â€” generate 5-second sample
- Default voice stored in `brand-kit.json` under `voiceover.defaultVoice`

### 9.2 Caption Generation

**Transcription pipeline** using Remotion's built-in Whisper integration (`@remotion/install-whisper-cpp`):

```
video:caption --input=voiceover.mp3
  â”œâ”€ 1. Install whisper.cpp model if not present (one-time)
  â”œâ”€ 2. Transcribe audio â†’ word-level timestamps
  â”œâ”€ 3. Generate SRT file
  â””â”€ 4. Return caption data for embedding in video
```

**Caption display styles** (using Remotion's subtitle rules):

| Style | Description | Best For |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| `word` | Word-by-word highlight (karaoke) | Reels, TikTok-style |
| `sentence` | Full sentence with fade timing | Professional, explainers |
| `block` | Multi-line subtitle block | Longer narration |

**Integration with templates:**
- Phase 2 templates (`talking-head`, `story-narrated`, `explainer-voiced`) accept a `captions` prop
- Caption component respects brand kit fonts and colors
- Caption positioning configurable: bottom, center, top

### 9.3 TTS + Caption Combined Workflow

```
video:generate --template=talking-head --script="..." --voice=rachel --captions=word
  â”œâ”€ 1. Generate voiceover from script via TTS provider
  â”œâ”€ 2. Transcribe voiceover audio via Whisper â†’ word timestamps
  â”œâ”€ 3. Build template props with audio path + caption data
  â”œâ”€ 4. Calculate video duration from audio duration
  â”œâ”€ 5. Preview â†’ Render â†’ Output
  â””â”€ Duration matches voiceover exactly (no manual timing needed)
```

## 10. Cloud Rendering (Phase 3)

### 10.1 Remotion Lambda Setup

```
video:cloud-init --region=us-east-1
  â”œâ”€ 1. Install @remotion/lambda
  â”œâ”€ 2. Validate AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)
  â”œâ”€ 3. Deploy Lambda function: npx remotion lambda functions deploy
  â”œâ”€ 4. Create S3 bucket for assets: npx remotion lambda sites create
  â”œâ”€ 5. Store config in video-studio/cloud-config.json:
  â”‚     { "functionName": "...", "region": "...", "s3Bucket": "..." }
  â”œâ”€ 6. Test render with a simple composition
  â””â”€ 7. Report: function ARN, estimated cost per render
```

### 10.2 Cloud Render Flow

```
video:generate --cloud
  â”œâ”€ Same steps 1-4 as local (parse, props, validate, preview locally)
  â”œâ”€ 5. Upload site bundle to S3 (if changed since last deploy)
  â”œâ”€ 6. Show cost estimate: "~$0.02 for a 30s video at 1080p"
  â”œâ”€ 7. Confirm with user
  â”œâ”€ 8. Invoke: npx remotion lambda render --function-name=<fn> ...
  â”œâ”€ 9. Poll progress (Lambda provides render progress %)
  â”œâ”€ 10. Download rendered file from S3
  â””â”€ 11. Clean up temporary S3 objects
```

### 10.3 Batch Rendering

```
video:batch --template=social-reel --presets=reel,square,landscape --cloud
  â”œâ”€ Spawn 3 parallel Lambda renders (same composition, different resolutions)
  â”œâ”€ Each render runs independently on separate Lambda invocations
  â”œâ”€ Download all 3 variants when complete
  â””â”€ Report: all file paths, total render time, total cost
```

### 10.4 Cost Estimation

Provide cost estimate before every cloud render:

| Video Length | Resolution | Estimated Cost |
|-------------|-----------|----------------|
| 15s | 1080p | ~$0.01 |
| 30s | 1080p | ~$0.02 |
| 60s | 1080p | ~$0.04 |
| 60s | 4K | ~$0.15 |

Estimates based on Remotion Lambda pricing (AWS Lambda compute time).

## 11. Custom Template Creation (Phase 3)

### 11.1 Template Scaffolding

```
video:template-create --name=webinar-intro --category=marketing --base=explainer
  â”œâ”€ 1. Copy base template as starting point
  â”œâ”€ 2. Generate new TypeScript interface for props
  â”œâ”€ 3. Register in Root.tsx
  â”œâ”€ 4. Create placeholder component
  â””â”€ 5. Open Remotion Studio for live preview editing:
        npx remotion studio (launches localhost:3000)
```

### 11.2 Template Development Workflow

1. User runs `video:template-create` to scaffold
2. Remotion Studio opens for interactive editing (hot-reload, timeline scrubbing)
3. User can ask Claude to modify the template code (agent reads `@remotion/skills` rules for guidance)
4. Test renders via Studio's built-in render button
5. Template becomes available in `video:templates` list

## 12. Phasing Summary

### Phase 1 â€” MVP (Core Video Generation)

**Deliverables:**
- `video:init` â€” project scaffolding with all dependencies
- `video:generate` â€” full generate pipeline with template selection
- `video:preview` â€” still frame preview before render
- `video:render` â€” re-render with updated props
- `video:templates` â€” template listing and details
- `video:brand` â€” brand kit management
- 8 starter templates (4 social + 4 marketing)
- 4 FOS skills (video-generation, video-templates, video-rendering, brand-kit)
- Stock asset bundle (music, SFX, textures)
- Local H.264/GIF rendering via Remotion CLI
- 5 output presets (reel, story, square, landscape, gif)
- Preflight checks (Node, ffmpeg, project, disk)
- Memory integration (observation logging)

**Dependencies:**
- Remotion v4+, Node.js 18+, ffmpeg
- `@remotion/skills` installed in managed project

### Phase 2 â€” TTS & Captions

**Deliverables:**
- `video:voiceover` â€” TTS generation (ElevenLabs or OpenAI)
- `video:caption` â€” Whisper transcription + SRT output
- 3 new templates (talking-head, story-narrated, explainer-voiced)
- 2 new skills (voiceover, captions)
- `social:compose --format=video` bridge to content pipeline
- Caption component with 3 display styles (word, sentence, block)
- Auto-sync: voiceover duration drives video duration

**Dependencies:**
- TTS provider API key (ElevenLabs or OpenAI)
- `@remotion/install-whisper-cpp` for transcription
- Phase 1 complete

### Phase 3 â€” Cloud & Custom Templates

**Deliverables:**
- `video:cloud-init` â€” Remotion Lambda deployment
- `video:batch` â€” parallel multi-preset rendering
- `video:template-create` â€” custom template scaffolding
- Cloud render support with cost estimation
- Batch rendering for platform variants
- Remotion Studio integration for template editing

**Dependencies:**
- AWS account with Lambda + S3 permissions
- `@remotion/lambda` package
- Phase 1 complete (Phase 2 not required)

## 13. Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Remotion project size (~200MB with node_modules) | Disk space on founder's machine | Document size in `video:init` output, support `--path` for external drives |
| Local render speed on weak hardware | 60s video could take 5+ minutes | Preview-first workflow. Phase 3 cloud rendering offloads compute. `--concurrency=50%` default to avoid CPU lockup |
| Template React code errors from user modifications | Broken renders | Templates are code-reviewed before shipping. Custom templates (Phase 3) render in Studio first |
| ffmpeg not installed | Blocking dependency | Clear install instructions in preflight failure message. Link to platform-specific guides |
| TTS API costs (Phase 2) | Recurring cost per voiceover | Show cost estimate before generation. Cache voiceovers for re-renders |
| `@remotion/skills` package changes | Could break FOS skill references | Pin version in package.json. Test on upgrade |
| Remotion license | Remotion requires a license for companies with >$10M revenue | Document in `video:init` output. Most founders under threshold. Link to remotion.dev/license |

## 14. Success Criteria

- [ ] `video:init` scaffolds a working Remotion project in < 2 minutes
- [ ] `video:generate` produces a branded video from script input in < 3 minutes (local, 30s video)
- [ ] Preview workflow shows 3 stills in < 10 seconds
- [ ] All 8 starter templates render without errors
- [ ] Brand kit applies consistently across all templates
- [ ] `social:compose --format=video` produces and uploads a video end-to-end (Phase 2)
- [ ] Cloud render completes in < 60 seconds for a 30s video (Phase 3)
- [ ] Template props are fully type-safe (TypeScript compile check)

# [20] Remotion Skill Design

> **Status:** Complete â€” awaiting review
> **Date:** 2026-03-19
> **Approach:** Template-Driven Pipeline (Approach A)

## 1. Overview

Claude Code skill for short-form and marketing video creation with Remotion. Enables founders to generate, preview, and render branded video content directly from Claude Code using the Remotion framework â€” React components driven by frame-based animation.

**Target use cases:**
- **Social content factory:** Reels, stories, quote cards, listicles for LinkedIn/X/Instagram
- **Marketing assets:** Product demos, testimonials, explainer videos, pitch highlights

**Core principle:** Composition-per-template. Each video type is a React composition with typed props. FOS generates the props from user input + brand kit, renders locally via Remotion CLI, and optionally uploads via the existing media-handler-agent pipeline.

## 2. Architecture

### 2.1 Managed Remotion Project

A self-contained Remotion project scaffolded once via `video:init` at a configurable path (default: `~/.founder-os/video-studio/`). The user never needs to touch it directly.

```
~/.founder-os/video-studio/
  â”œâ”€â”€ src/
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Root.tsx              # Composition registry (all templates registered here)
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ templates/            # One React component per template
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialReel.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialQuote.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialListicle.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SocialBeforeAfter.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ ProductDemo.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Testimonial.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Explainer.tsx
  â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ PitchHighlight.tsx
  â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ components/           # Shared building blocks
  â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ TextOverlay.tsx   # Animated text with brand fonts

### 2.2 Data Flow
